=== metaclass ===
class UOpMetaClass(type):
  ucache:dict[tuple, weakref.ReferenceType[UOp]] = {}
  def __call__(cls, op:Ops, dtype:DType=dtypes.void, src:tuple[UOp,...]=tuple(), arg:Any=None, tag:Any=None,
               metadata:tuple[Metadata,...]|None=None, _buffer:Buffer|None=None):
    if (wret:=UOpMetaClass.ucache.get(key:=(op, dtype, src, arg, tag), None)) is not None and (ret:=wret()) is not None: return ret
    UOpMetaClass.ucache[key] = weakref.ref(created:=super().__call__(*key))
    if metadata is not None: all_metadata[created] = metadata
    # NOTE: this value is set by pickle when pickling a realized tensor
    if _buffer is not None:
      assert op is Ops.BUFFER, f"trying to set Buffer {_buffer} for {op}"
      buffers[created] = _buffer
    if SPEC > 1:
      from tinygrad.uop.spec import full_spec, test_pyrender
      if SPEC > 2:
        # SPEC=3 checks the shape
        _ = created._shape
        if SPEC > 3:
          test_pyrender(created)
      with Context(CHECK_OOB=0): fret = cast(bool|None, full_spec.rewrite(created))
      if fret is not True: raise RuntimeError(f"SPEC ISSUE {fret}: {created}")
    return created

# some uops map to other stuff
buffers:weakref.WeakKeyDictionary[UOp, Buffer|MultiBuffer] = weakref.WeakKeyDictionary() # this maps BUFFER/BUFFER_VIEW uops to their device Buffers
all_metadata:weakref.WeakKeyDictionary[UOp, tuple[Metadata, ...]] = weakref.WeakKeyDictionary() # TODO: should this be here?

# recursive_property replaces functools.cached_property in recursive UOp functions to prevent RecursionError
class recursive_property(property):
  def __init__(self, fxn):
    self.fxn = fxn
    self.nm = "_RECURSIVE_PROPERTY_"+fxn.__name__
    self.__doc__ = fxn.__doc__
  def __get__(self, x:UOp|None, owner=None):
    if x is None: return self
    if self.nm in x.__dict__: return x.__dict__[self.nm]
    for node in x.toposort(gate=lambda node: self.nm not in node.__dict__): node.__dict__[self.nm] = self.fxn(node)
    return x.__dict__[self.nm]

# we import this late so we can use resolve/smax in mixins
from tinygrad.mixin import OpMixin

# NOTE: this should be frozen, but frozen is slower
@dataclass(eq=False, slots=True)
class UOp(OpMixin, metaclass=UOpMetaClass):
  op:Ops
  dtype:DType = dtypes.void
  src:tuple[UOp, ...] = tuple()
  arg:Any = None
  tag:Any = None
  def __del__(self):

class UOpMetaClass(type):
  ucache:dict[tuple, weakref.ReferenceType[UOp]] = {}
  def __call__(cls, op:Ops, dtype:DType=dtypes.void, src:tuple[UOp,...]=tuple(), arg:Any=None, tag:Any=None,
               metadata:tuple[Metadata,...]|None=None, _buffer:Buffer|None=None):
    if (wret:=UOpMetaClass.ucache.get(key:=(op, dtype, src, arg, tag), None)) is not None and (ret:=wret()) is not None: return ret
    UOpMetaClass.ucache[key] = weakref.ref(created:=super().__call__(*key))
    if metadata is not None: all_metadata[created] = metadata
    # NOTE: this value is set by pickle when pickling a realized tensor
    if _buffer is not None:
      assert op is Ops.BUFFER, f"trying to set Buffer {_buffer} for {op}"
      buffers[created] = _buffer
    if SPEC > 1:
      from tinygrad.uop.spec import full_spec, test_pyrender
      if SPEC > 2:
        # SPEC=3 checks the shape
        _ = created._shape
        if SPEC > 3:
          test_pyrender(created)
      with Context(CHECK_OOB=0): fret = cast(bool|None, full_spec.rewrite(created))
      if fret is not True: raise RuntimeError(f"SPEC ISSUE {fret}: {created}")
    return created

# some uops map to other stuff
buffers:weakref.WeakKeyDictionary[UOp, Buffer|MultiBuffer] = weakref.WeakKeyDictionary() # this maps BUFFER/BUFFER_VIEW uops to their device Buffers
all_metadata:weakref.WeakKeyDictionary[UOp, tuple[Metadata, ...]] = weakref.WeakKeyDictionary() # TODO: should this be here?

# recursive_property replaces functools.cached_property in recursive UOp functions to prevent RecursionError
class recursive_property(property):
  def __init__(self, fxn):
    self.fxn = fxn
    self.nm = "_RECURSIVE_PROPERTY_"+fxn.__name__
    self.__doc__ = fxn.__doc__
  def __get__(self, x:UOp|None, owner=None):
    if x is None: return self
    if self.nm in x.__dict__: return x.__dict__[self.nm]
    for node in x.toposort(gate=lambda node: self.nm not in node.__dict__): node.__dict__[self.nm] = self.fxn(node)
    return x.__dict__[self.nm]

# we import this late so we can use resolve/smax in mixins
from tinygrad.mixin import OpMixin

# NOTE: this should be frozen, but frozen is slower
@dataclass(eq=False, slots=True)
class UOp(OpMixin, metaclass=UOpMetaClass):
  op:Ops
  dtype:DType = dtypes.void
  src:tuple[UOp, ...] = tuple()
  arg:Any = None
  tag:Any = None
  def __del__(self):

```python
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  if y_min==y_max==0: raise ZeroDivisionError(...)
  if y_min*y_max > 0 and (qv:=floordiv(x_min,y_min)) == floordiv(x_min,y_max) == floordiv(x_max,y_min) == floordiv(x_max,y_max):
    return x - qv*y if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD else d.const_like(qv)
```

The condition that floordiv(x_min,...) == floordiv(x_max,...) for all combinations ensures the whole [x_min,x_max] maps to same quotient. Use floordiv instead of cdiv. ✓.

```python
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  if y_min==y_max==0: raise ZeroDivisionError(...)
  if y_min*y_max > 0 and (qv:=floordiv(x_min,y_min)) == floordiv(x_min,y_max) == floordiv(x_max,y_min) == floordiv(x_max,y_max):
    return x - qv*y if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD else d.const_like(qv)
```

The condition that floordiv(x_min,...) == floordiv(x_max,...) for all combinations ensures the whole [x_min,x_max] maps to same quotient. Use floordiv instead of cdiv. ✓.

```python
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax

    return x - qv*y if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD else d.const_like(qv)
```

from typing import Callable, cast
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, GroupOp, Ops, UOp, python_alu
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes, Invalid
from tinygrad.helpers import cpu_profile
import z3

# older versions of z3 dont have some operators like & overloaded
if z3.get_version() < (4, 12, 4, 0):
  raise ImportError("bounds checking requires z3 >= 4.12.4, use CHECK_OOB=0 to disable, or \"pip install 'z3-solver>=4.12.4\"")

# IDIV is truncated division but z3 does euclidian division (floor if b>0 ceil otherwise); mod by power of two sometimes uses Ops.AND
def z3_cdiv(a:z3.ArithRef, b:z3.ArithRef) -> z3.ArithRef:return z3.If((a<0), z3.If(0<b, (a+(b-1))/b, (a-(b+1))/b), a/b)
def z3_floordiv(a:z3.ArithRef, b:z3.ArithRef) -> z3.ArithRef: return z3.If(b > 0, a/b, (-a)/(-b))
def z3_xor(a:z3.ExprRef, b:z3.ExprRef) -> z3.ExprRef:
  if isinstance(a, z3.BoolRef): return a^b
  # x ^ -1 = -(x+1), i.e. bitwise NOT
  if isinstance(b, z3.IntNumRef) and b.as_long() == -1: return -(a+1)
  if isinstance(a, z3.IntNumRef) and a.as_long() == -1: return -(b+1)
  raise RuntimeError(f"z3 int XOR only supports XOR with -1, got {a=} {b=}")
z3_alu: dict[Ops, Callable[..., z3.ExprRef]] = python_alu | {Ops.MOD: lambda a,b: a-z3_cdiv(a,b)*b, Ops.IDIV: z3_cdiv, Ops.FLOORDIV: z3_floordiv,
  Ops.FLOORMOD: lambda a,b: a-z3_floordiv(a,b)*b,
  Ops.SHR: lambda a,b: a/(2**b.as_long()), Ops.SHL: lambda a,b: a*(2**b.as_long()),
  Ops.AND: lambda a,b: a%(b+1) if isinstance(b, z3.ArithRef) else a&b, Ops.WHERE: z3.If, Ops.XOR: z3_xor, Ops.MAX: lambda a,b: z3.If(a<b, b, a),}
def create_bounded(name:str, vmin:int, vmax:int, z3ctx:z3.Context) -> tuple[z3.ArithRef, z3.BoolRef]:
  return (s:=z3.Int(name, ctx=z3ctx)), (vmin <= s)&(s <= vmax)

z3_renderer = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat.var("cond").where(UPat.var("x"), UPat.const(dtypes.weakint, Invalid)), lambda x,cond,ctx: (ctx[1][x], ctx[1][cond])),
  # variables
  (UPat(Ops.SPECIAL, name="x"), lambda x,ctx: create_bounded(x.arg, 0, ctx[1][x.src[0]]-1, ctx[0])),

from typing import Callable, cast
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, GroupOp, Ops, UOp, python_alu
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes, Invalid
from tinygrad.helpers import cpu_profile
import z3

# older versions of z3 dont have some operators like & overloaded
if z3.get_version() < (4, 12, 4, 0):
  raise ImportError("bounds checking requires z3 >= 4.12.4, use CHECK_OOB=0 to disable, or \"pip install 'z3-solver>=4.12.4\"")

# IDIV is truncated division but z3 does euclidian division (floor if b>0 ceil otherwise); mod by power of two sometimes uses Ops.AND
def z3_cdiv(a:z3.ArithRef, b:z3.ArithRef) -> z3.ArithRef:return z3.If((a<0), z3.If(0<b, (a+(b-1))/b, (a-(b+1))/b), a/b)
def z3_floordiv(a:z3.ArithRef, b:z3.ArithRef) -> z3.ArithRef: return z3.If(b > 0, a/b, (-a)/(-b))
def z3_xor(a:z3.ExprRef, b:z3.ExprRef) -> z3.ExprRef:
  if isinstance(a, z3.BoolRef): return a^b
  # x ^ -1 = -(x+1), i.e. bitwise NOT
  if isinstance(b, z3.IntNumRef) and b.as_long() == -1: return -(a+1)
  if isinstance(a, z3.IntNumRef) and a.as_long() == -1: return -(b+1)
  raise RuntimeError(f"z3 int XOR only supports XOR with -1, got {a=} {b=}")
z3_alu: dict[Ops, Callable[..., z3.ExprRef]] = python_alu | {Ops.MOD: lambda a,b: a-z3_cdiv(a,b)*b, Ops.IDIV: z3_cdiv, Ops.FLOORDIV: z3_floordiv,
  Ops.FLOORMOD: lambda a,b: a-z3_floordiv(a,b)*b,
  Ops.SHR: lambda a,b: a/(2**b.as_long()), Ops.SHL: lambda a,b: a*(2**b.as_long()),
  Ops.AND: lambda a,b: a%(b+1) if isinstance(b, z3.ArithRef) else a&b, Ops.WHERE: z3.If, Ops.XOR: z3_xor, Ops.MAX: lambda a,b: z3.If(a<b, b, a),}
def create_bounded(name:str, vmin:int, vmax:int, z3ctx:z3.Context) -> tuple[z3.ArithRef, z3.BoolRef]:
  return (s:=z3.Int(name, ctx=z3ctx)), (vmin <= s)&(s <= vmax)

z3_renderer = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat.var("cond").where(UPat.var("x"), UPat.const(dtypes.weakint, Invalid)), lambda x,cond,ctx: (ctx[1][x], ctx[1][cond])),
  # variables
  (UPat(Ops.SPECIAL, name="x"), lambda x,ctx: create_bounded(x.arg, 0, ctx[1][x.src[0]]-1, ctx[0])),
  (UPat(Ops.DEFINE_VAR, name="x"), lambda x,ctx: create_bounded(x.arg[0], x.arg[1], x.arg[2], ctx[0])),
  (UPat(Ops.RANGE, name="x"), lambda x,ctx: create_bounded(x.render(simplify=False), 0, ctx[1][x.src[0]]-1, ctx[0])),
  # loads are variables bounded by the min/max of the dtype. non-pointer INDEX is also a LOAD
  (UPat((Ops.LOAD, Ops.INDEX), dtypes.ints+(dtypes.weakint,), name="x"), lambda x,ctx:
    create_bounded(f"load{len(ctx[1])}", x.dtype.min, x.dtype.max, ctx[0])),
  (UPat((Ops.LOAD, Ops.INDEX), dtypes.bool), lambda ctx: (z3.Bool(f"load{len(ctx[1])}", ctx=ctx[0]), None)),
  # constants
  (UPat(Ops.CONST, arg=Invalid), lambda ctx: (z3.Int("Invalid", ctx=ctx[0]), None)),
  (UPat(Ops.CONST, dtypes.ints+(dtypes.weakint,), name="x"), lambda x,ctx: (z3.IntVal(x.arg, ctx=ctx[0]), None)),
  (UPat(Ops.CONST, dtypes.bool, name="x"), lambda x,ctx: (z3.BoolVal(x.arg, ctx=ctx[0]), None)),

messages.reasoning_content:
The validate and spec already handle FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. Good.

Now I need to understand: are there places that currently rely on `//`/`%` producing IDIV/MOD for index arithmetic in the schedule/codegen? If I switch `__floordiv__` and `__mod__` to FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, then all index arithmetic in tensor.py / shape logic would produce FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, which then get lowered to IDIV/MOD by late_rewrite. This should generally work since the lowering exists.

But there might be places that pattern-match on `Ops.IDIV`/`Ops.MOD` expecting them from `//`/`%`. Let me search for usages in the schedule and codegen that match IDIV/MOD.

The codegen/simplify.py has `count_divmod` checking `Ops.IDIV, Ops.MOD`. After our change, index arithmetic would produce FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, but these get lowered to IDIV/MOD in late_rewrite which runs... when? Let me check the codegen pipeline order. `get_late_rewrite_patterns` is called with `symbolic_simple+get_late_rewrite_patterns(supported_ops, ...)`. So late rewrite lowers FLOORDIV->IDIV. After that, count_divmod (which counts IDIV/MOD) would still work since they're lowered.

But the symbolic simplification (`div_and_mod_symbolic`) runs in `symbolic_simple` which is BEFORE late_rewrite. So we need div_and_mod_symbolic to handle FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. That's our change. Good.

Let me check `symbolic_simple` composition.

import itertools
from typing import Callable
from tinygrad.uop.ops import UOp, PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, graph_rewrite, _substitute, range_start, AxisType
from tinygrad.uop.symbolic import symbolic
from tinygrad.helpers import partition
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes

def flatten_range(r:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  off = range_start[r.op]
  rngs = r.src[off:]
  if not len(rngs): return None
  new_rngs = [x for x in UOp.sink(*rngs).toposort() if x.op is Ops.RANGE]
  return r.replace(src=r.src[:off]+tuple(new_rngs))

pm_flatten_range = PatternMatcher([
  # real ranges only
  (UPat((Ops.REDUCE, Ops.END), name="r"), flatten_range),
])

def count_divmod(x:UOp) -> int: return sum(u.op in {Ops.IDIV, Ops.MOD} for u in x.backward_slice)
def simplify_merge_adjacent(u:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  reduce_ranges = [x.ranges for x in u.backward_slice_with_self if x.op is Ops.REDUCE]
  # on END we only want to merge adjacent ranges, on REDUCE we want to try all combinations

extra.raw_output:
import itertools
from typing import Callable
from tinygrad.uop.ops import UOp, PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, graph_rewrite, _substitute, range_start, AxisType
from tinygrad.uop.symbolic import symbolic
from tinygrad.helpers import partition
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes

def flatten_range(r:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  off = range_start[r.op]
  rngs = r.src[off:]
  if not len(rngs): return None
  new_rngs = [x for x in UOp.sink(*rngs).toposort() if x.op is Ops.RANGE]
  return r.replace(src=r.src[:off]+tuple(new_rngs))

pm_flatten_range = PatternMatcher([
  # real ranges only
  (UPat((Ops.REDUCE, Ops.END), name="r"), flatten_range),
])

def count_divmod(x:UOp) -> int: return sum(u.op in {Ops.IDIV, Ops.MOD} for u in x.backward_slice)
def simplify_merge_adjacent(u:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  reduce_ranges = [x.ranges for x in u.backward_slice_with_self if x.op is Ops.REDUCE]
  # on END we only want to merge adjacent ranges, on REDUCE we want to try all combinations
  for r0, r1 in (zip(u.ended_ranges, u.ended_ranges[1:]) if u.op is Ops.END else itertools.permutations(u.ended_ranges, 2)):
    # check same type
    if r0.arg[-1] == r1.arg[-1]:
      # check if the ranges to merge are in the same reduces
      if all((r0 in rngs) == (r1 in rngs) for rngs in reduce_ranges):
        s0, s1 = r0.src[0], r1.src[0]
        # do the merge
        new_range = r0.replace(src=(s0*s1,))
        nidx = graph_rewrite(u, _substitute+symbolic+pm_flatten_range, ctx={r0:new_range//s1, r1:new_range%s1},
                             name=f"check_merge_{r0.arg[0]}_{r1.arg[0]}")

        # check if it simplifies
        if count_divmod(nidx) <= count_divmod(u):
          u = nidx
  return u

def mark_gated(ctx, idx):
===codegen init===
  # create image buffers
  if IMAGE and ren.target.device in {"QCOM", "CL", "PYTHON"}: sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_make_images, name="create image buffers", bottom_up=True)

  # devectorize (TODO: does this need opts?)
  if DEVECTORIZE >= 2: pm_devectorize = sym+load_store_folding+load_store_indexing
  elif DEVECTORIZE: pm_devectorize = sym+devectorize+load_store_folding+correct_load_store+load_store_indexing
  else: pm_devectorize = sym+load_store_folding+correct_load_store+load_store_indexing
  if DEVECTORIZE >= 0: sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_devectorize, ctx=ren, name="devectorize")

  # lower the index dtype to a concrete int
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_lower_index_dtype+load_store_indexing+gep_pushing, name="lower all index dtypes")
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, symbolic, name="post index symbolic")

  # optional pre matcher
  if ren.pre_matcher is not None: sink = graph_rewrite(sink, ren.pre_matcher, name="pre_matcher")

  # decompositions
  supported_ops = tuple(ren.code_for_op.keys())
  pm_decomp = symbolic_simple+get_late_rewrite_patterns(supported_ops, bool(DISABLE_FAST_IDIV))
  pm_transcendental = symbolic_simple+get_transcendental_patterns(supported_ops, TRANSCENDENTAL>=2)
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_decomp, ctx=ren.target, name="decompositions")
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_dtype_decomps, ctx=(set(), ren.target), name="decomp dtypes")
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_transcendental, name="transcendental")

  # final rules for the renderer (without sym)
  extra_matcher = ren.extra_matcher if ren.extra_matcher is not None else PatternMatcher([])
  pm_final_rewrite = pm_decomp+pm_render+extra_matcher+pm_split_ends
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_final_rewrite, ctx=ren.target, name="final rewrite")

  # this was the linearizer
  sink = graph_rewrite(sink, pm_add_control_flow, ctx=CFGContext(sink), name="add control flow", bottom_up=True)

Now, the `fold_add_divmod_recombine` in symbolic.py matches `Ops.MOD`/`Ops.IDIV`. Since `//` and `%` would now produce FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, this recombine rule needs to match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD too. Let me update it.

Also `count_divmod` in codegen/simplify.py counts IDIV/MOD. After our change, the index expressions would be FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD during symbolic, then lowered to IDIV/MOD by late_rewrite. But `simplify_merge_adjacent` runs with `symbolic` (which includes div_and_mod_symbolic). At that point, are the ops FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD or IDIV/MOD? They'd be FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD (produced by `//`/`%`). The `new_range//s1` and `new_range%s1` use `//` and `%` → FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So count_divmod counting IDIV/MOD would count 0 for both... that breaks the merge logic (it would always think simplification happened). 

Hmm. So count_divmod should also count FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. Let me update it to count all four. Actually, let me think — at the point simplify_merge_adjacent runs, the index arithmetic uses `//`/`%` which now produce FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So count_divmod should count FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. Let me update it.

But wait — simplify_merge_adjacent is in codegen/simplify.py which is a test/non-test? It's source. I can modify it. Let me update count_divmod to include FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD.

Actually, let me reconsider. The whole pipeline is complex. Let me think about whether switching `__floordiv__`/`__mod__` globally is too disruptive. 

Actually, the issue explicitly says to do this. And the existing test `test_uop_resolve.test_rfloordiv`: `8 // UOp.const(dtypes.int, 4)` expects 2. With FLOORDIV, floordiv(8,4)=2. ✓. And `__rmod__` etc.

Let me also check the `is_increasing` and `_min_max` already handle FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. Yes they do (lines 866-873).

Now let me think about the `fold_add_divmod_recombine`. It currently matches MOD/IDIV. With `//`/`%` producing FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, this rule won't fire. But the rule's purpose is to recombine `(base%div)*mul + (base//div)*(div*mul) -> base*mul`. For floor this identity holds (x = floordiv(x,y)*y + floormod(x,y)). So update to match FLOORMOD/FLOORDIV.

But wait — at the time `fold_add_divmod_recombine` runs (it's in `symbolic_simple`), the ops would be FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD (if produced by `//`/`%`). But could there still be IDIV/MOD from elsewhere? The `idiv()` method still produces IDIV. And renderers/late produce IDIV/MOD. Hmm. But `symbolic_simple` runs early on tensor graph where `//`/`%` produce FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. Let me make fold_add_divmod_recombine match both sets to be safe. Actually, let me match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD primarily but also keep IDIV/MOD for compatibility (since idiv() still exists and lowers). Hmm, but if I match both, the recombine for IDIV/MOD (trunc) is only correct when base>=0... The existing rule doesn't check that. It was already potentially incorrect for trunc with negatives, but presumably only used in non-negative contexts. 

Let me make fold_add_divmod_recombine match FLOORDIV and FLOORMOD (the new floor ops). Keep it simple.

Actually wait, there's a subtlety. The `fold_add_divmod_recombine` checks `q.op is Ops.IDIV` and `base.op is Ops.IDIV` and `q.op is Ops.MOD`. I need to change these to FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. But should I also handle IDIV/MOD? Let me think about whether any IDIV/MOD reach symbolic_simple. The `idiv()` tensor method produces IDIV. Tensor.idiv is used... where? Let me not worry; let me match both FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD and keep IDIV/MOD matching too for robustness. Actually, mixing could cause incorrect recombination for trunc. But the existing code already did trunc recombination without guards, so it's no worse. Let me match both: `{Ops.IDIV, Ops.FLOORDIV}` and `{Ops.MOD, Ops.FLOORMOD}`.

Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me be careful and check: the recombine for floor: `(base%div)*mul + (base//div)*(div*mul) -> base*mul`. For floor this is exactly the identity. For trunc: `cmod(base,div)*mul + cdiv(base,div)*div*mul = base*mul`? cmod + cdiv*div = base (trunc identity). Yes holds for trunc too. So matching both is fine and correct. Good.

Let me now also check the `gated_given_valid` and `IMAGE` check `op_in_backward_slice_with_self(Ops.IDIV, Ops.MOD)`. Should this include FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD? It's about image indexing with div/mod. After our change, image indices use FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So yes, update to include them. Let me update.

OK. This is a big but doable change. Let me also check usages of `.mod()` and `.idiv()` and `//`/`%` across the codebase that might break. Let me search tensor.py and schedule for index arithmetic.

Actually, given budget, let me be efficient. Let me now look at the symbolic.py divmod-related sections fully (lines 76-300, 400-415).

<output>
symbolic_simple = propagate_invalid + PatternMatcher([
  # ** self folding **
  (UPat.var("x") + 0, lambda x: x),    # x+0 -> x
  (UPat.var("x") * 1, lambda x: x),    # x*1 -> x
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.ints+(dtypes.bool, dtypes.weakint)) ^ 0, lambda x: x), # x^0 -> x
  (UPat.var("x") // UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(1)), # x//x -> 1
  (UPat.var("x") // 1, lambda x: x),   # x//1 -> x
  (UPat.var("x") // -1, lambda x: -x), # x//-1 -> -x
  ((UPat.var("x") ^ UPat.var("y")) ^ UPat.var("y"), lambda x,y: x), # (x^y)^y -> x
  ((UPat.var() % UPat.var("y")).named("base") % UPat.var("y"), lambda base,y: base),  # (x%y)%y = -> x%y (rewritten with base for speed)
  # variations of (x%c)+(x//c)*c = x
  (UPat(Ops.ADD, dtype=dtypes.weakint, name="x"), fold_add_divmod_recombine),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool) & UPat.cvar("c", vec=False), lambda x,c: x if c.arg else c),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool) | UPat.cvar("c", vec=False), lambda x,c: c if c.arg else x),
  (UPat(GroupOp.Idempotent, src=(UPat.var("x"), UPat.var("x"))), lambda x: x),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool).logical_not().logical_not(), lambda x: x),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool).where(UPat.const(dtypes.bool, True), UPat.const(dtypes.bool, False)), lambda x: x),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool).where(UPat.const(dtypes.bool, False), UPat.const(dtypes.bool, True)), lambda x: x.logical_not()),
  # CAST(bool -> int) != const — CAST(True)=1, CAST(False)=0, so fold based on const value
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.bool).cast(dtypes.ints+(dtypes.weakint,)) != UPat.cvar("c", vec=False),
   lambda x,c: x if c.arg == 0 else x.logical_not() if c.arg == 1 else x.const_like(True)),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.ints+(dtypes.bool, dtypes.weakint)).trunc(), lambda x: x),
  # ** zero folding **
  (UPat.var("x") < UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(False).cast(dtypes.bool.vec(x.dtype.count))), # x < x -> False
  (UPat.var("x") % UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(0)), # x%x -> 0
  (UPat.var("x") ^ UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(0)), # x^x -> 0
  (UPat.var("x") & 0, lambda x: x.const_like(0)), # x&0 -> 0
  # (x&mask)>>k -> x>>k when mask only clears bits below k
  # TODO: combine this with "# rules for threefry" below
  ((UPat.var("x") & UPat.cvar("mask", vec=False)) >> UPat.cvar("k", vec=False),
   lambda x,mask,k: x >> k.arg if mask.arg | ((1 << k.arg) - 1) == -1 else None),
  (UPat.var("x", dtype=dtypes.ints+(dtypes.bool, dtypes.weakint)) != UPat.var("x"),
   lambda x: x.const_like(False).cast(dtypes.bool.vec(x.dtype.count))), # x != x -> False (only ints)
  # ** constant folding **
  (UPat(GroupOp.Unary, src=(UPat((Ops.VCONST, Ops.CONST)),), name="a"), lambda a: a.const_like(exec_alu(a.op, a.dtype, [a.src[0].arg], False))),
  (UPat(GroupOp.Binary-{Ops.THREEFRY}, src=(UPat((Ops.VCONST, Ops.CONST)),)*2, name="a"),
   lambda a: a.const_like(exec_alu(a.op, a.dtype, [a.src[0].arg, a.src[1].arg], False))),
  (UPat(Ops.THREEFRY, src=(UPat.cvar("x"), UPat.cvar("key")), name="a"),
   lambda a, x, key: a.const_like(threefry2x32(x, key).simplify().arg)),
  (UPat(GroupOp.Ternary, src=(UPat((Ops.VCONST, Ops.CONST)),)*3, name="a"),
   lambda a: a.const_like(exec_alu(a.op, a.dtype, [a.src[0].arg, a.src[1].arg, a.src[2].arg], False))),
  # bool MUL is AND, ADD/MAX is OR. prevents other rules to rewrite bool ADD/MUL incorrectly
  (UPat.var('x', dtype=dtypes.bool) * UPat.var('y', dtype=dtypes.bool), lambda x,y: x&y),
  (UPat.var('x', dtype=dtypes.bool) + UPat.var('y', dtype=dtypes.bool), lambda x,y: x|y),
  (UPat.var('x', dtype=dtypes.bool).maximum(UPat.var('y', dtype=dtypes.bool)), lambda x,y: x|y),
  # *** div rules ***
  (UPat.cvar('x', arg=0) / 0, lambda x: x.const_like(float('nan'))),   # 0/0 -> nan
  ((UPat.var("x") * 0) / 0, lambda x: x.const_like(float('nan'))),     # (x*0)/0 -> nan
  # can be wrong if x or x2 is 0
  (UPat.var("x") / UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(1)),          # x/x -> 1
  ((UPat.var("x") * UPat.var("x2")) / UPat.var("x2"), lambda x,x2: x), # (x*x2)/x2 -> x
  # x*0 -> 0 or 0*x -> 0
  # if x is nan or inf it should render the nan value.
  # NOTE: this can be wrong for loaded NaN
  (UPat.var("x") * 0, lambda x: x.const_like(float("nan") if x.op is Ops.CONST
                                             and isinstance(x.arg, float) and (math.isnan(x.arg) or math.isinf(x.arg)) else 0)),
  # *** cast/bitcast ***
  (UPat(Ops.CAST, name="root", src=(UPat.cvar("c"),)), lambda root, c: root.const_like(c.arg)),
  (UPat((Ops.CAST, Ops.BITCAST), name="root"), lambda root: root.src[0] if root.dtype == root.src[0].dtype else None),
  (UPat(Ops.BITCAST, name="root", src=(UPat.cvar("c"),)), fold_bitcast),
  # b.cast(a).cast(b) -> b if a preserves all values in b
  (UPat.var('x').cast(name="a").cast(name="b"), lambda x,a,b: x if x.dtype == b.dtype and can_lossless_cast(b.dtype, a.dtype) else None),
  (UPat.var("x").cast(dtypes.bool), lambda x: x != 0),
  # ** pow **
  (UPat.var("x").alu(Ops.POW, UPat.cvar("c", vec=False)), simplify_pow),
  # positive const ** x
  (UPat.cvar("c", vec=False).alu(Ops.POW, UPat.var("x")), lambda c,x: c if c.arg == 1 else (x*math.log2(c.arg)).exp2() if c.arg > 0 else None),
  # rules for threefry
  ((UPat.var('x', dtypes.uint64)&0xFFFFFFFF).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda x: x.cast(dtypes.uint32)),
  (((UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint64)*(1<<32)) | UPat.var('y',  dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64)).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda y: y),
  (((UPat.var('x',  dtypes.uint64)*(1<<32)) | UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64))//(1<<32), lambda x: x),
  (((UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint64)<<32) | UPat.var('y',  dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64)).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda y: y),
  (((UPat.var('x',  dtypes.uint64)<<32) | UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64))>>32, lambda x: x),
  # ** simple where folding **
  # a conditional with the same results either way is a noop, also fold const conditionals
  (UPat.var().where(UPat.var("val"), UPat.var("val")), lambda val: val),
  (UPat.cvar("gate", vec=False).where(UPat.var("c0"), UPat.var("c1")), lambda gate, c0, c1: c0 if gate.arg else c1),
  # a.where(b.where(c, d), d) -> (a & b).where(c, d)
  (UPat.var("a").where(UPat.var("b").where(UPat.var("c"), UPat.var("d")), UPat.var("d")), lambda a,b,c,d: (a&b).where(c,d)),
])

# ******** phase 2 builds on phase 1, it includes the old "symbolic", rules that match deeper ********

def lt_folding(x:UOp, c:int) -> UOp|None:
  p, np = partition(x.split_uop(Ops.ADD), lambda u: u.const_factor() == 1)
</output>

  (UPat.var("x") / UPat.var("x"), lambda x: x.const_like(1)),          # x/x -> 1
  ((UPat.var("x") * UPat.var("x2")) / UPat.var("x2"), lambda x,x2: x), # (x*x2)/x2 -> x
  # x*0 -> 0 or 0*x -> 0
  # if x is nan or inf it should render the nan value.
  # NOTE: this can be wrong for loaded NaN
  (UPat.var("x") * 0, lambda x: x.const_like(float("nan") if x.op is Ops.CONST
                                             and isinstance(x.arg, float) and (math.isnan(x.arg) or math.isinf(x.arg)) else 0)),
  # *** cast/bitcast ***
  (UPat(Ops.CAST, name="root", src=(UPat.cvar("c"),)), lambda root, c: root.const_like(c.arg)),
  (UPat((Ops.CAST, Ops.BITCAST), name="root"), lambda root: root.src[0] if root.dtype == root.src[0].dtype else None),
  (UPat(Ops.BITCAST, name="root", src=(UPat.cvar("c"),)), fold_bitcast),
  # b.cast(a).cast(b) -> b if a preserves all values in b
  (UPat.var('x').cast(name="a").cast(name="b"), lambda x,a,b: x if x.dtype == b.dtype and can_lossless_cast(b.dtype, a.dtype) else None),
  (UPat.var("x").cast(dtypes.bool), lambda x: x != 0),
  # ** pow **
  (UPat.var("x").alu(Ops.POW, UPat.cvar("c", vec=False)), simplify_pow),
  # positive const ** x
  (UPat.cvar("c", vec=False).alu(Ops.POW, UPat.var("x")), lambda c,x: c if c.arg == 1 else (x*math.log2(c.arg)).exp2() if c.arg > 0 else None),
  # rules for threefry
  ((UPat.var('x', dtypes.uint64)&0xFFFFFFFF).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda x: x.cast(dtypes.uint32)),
  (((UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint64)*(1<<32)) | UPat.var('y',  dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64)).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda y: y),
  (((UPat.var('x',  dtypes.uint64)*(1<<32)) | UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64))//(1<<32), lambda x: x),
  (((UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint64)<<32) | UPat.var('y',  dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64)).cast(dtypes.uint32), lambda y: y),
  (((UPat.var('x',  dtypes.uint64)<<32) | UPat.var(None, dtypes.uint32).cast(dtypes.uint64))>>32, lambda x: x),
  # ** simple where folding **
  # a conditional with the same results either way is a noop, also fold const conditionals
  (UPat.var().where(UPat.var("val"), UPat.var("val")), lambda val: val),
  (UPat.cvar("gate", vec=False).where(UPat.var("c0"), UPat.var("c1")), lambda gate, c0, c1: c0 if gate.arg else c1),
  # a.where(b.where(c, d), d) -> (a & b).where(c, d)
  (UPat.var("a").where(UPat.var("b").where(UPat.var("c"), UPat.var("d")), UPat.var("d")), lambda a,b,c,d: (a&b).where(c,d)),
])

# ******** phase 2 builds on phase 1, it includes the old "symbolic", rules that match deeper ********

def lt_folding(x:UOp, c:int) -> UOp|None:
  p, np = partition(x.split_uop(Ops.ADD), lambda u: u.const_factor() == 1)

pm_move_where_on_load = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat.var("cond").where(UPat.var("buf").index(UPat.var("idx")).or_casted("or_cast"), 0), where_on_load),
  (UPat.var("cond").where(0, UPat.var("buf").index(UPat.var("idx")).or_casted("or_cast")),
   lambda cond,buf,idx,or_cast: where_on_load(cond.logical_not(),buf,idx,or_cast)),
])

def gated_given_valid(cond:UOp, x:UOp, i:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  if x.dtype.scalar() is not dtypes.weakint: return None
  # Skip if x contains DIV/MOD AND IMAGE mode is enabled -> image index e.g. openpilot
  if IMAGE.value > 0 and x.op_in_backward_slice_with_self(Ops.IDIV, Ops.MOD): return None
  return cond.where(uop_given_valid(cond, x, try_simplex=False), i)

1. `UPat.var("x") // UPat.var("x") -> 1`: `x//x -> 1`. For floor: floordiv(x,x)=1 (when x!=0). ✓.
2. `UPat.var("x") // 1 -> x`: floordiv(x,1)=x ✓.
3. `UPat.var("x") // -1 -> -x`: floordiv(x,-1) = floor(x/-1) = floor(-x) = -x (since -x is integer). ✓.
4. `(x%y)%y -> x%y`: floormod(floormod(x,y),y) = floormod(x,y). ✓.
5. `x%x -> 0`: floormod(x,x)=0 ✓.
6. `(x//c1)//c2 -> x//(c1*c2)`: floordiv(floordiv(x,c1),c2) = floordiv(x, c1*c2) for positive c1,c2? floor(floor(x/c1)/c2) = floor(x/(c1*c2)) when c1,c2>0. ✓ (for positive). The cvars here could be negative though. Original rule had no sign guard. For floor with negative c1/c2 it may not hold. But the original (trunc) also didn't guard... Actually trunc: cdiv(cdiv(x,c1),c2)=cdiv(x,c1*c2)? Not always with negatives. Hmm, original didn't guard. Let me add c1,c2>0 guard? The original didn't, relying on... Actually let me keep it as is for now but it might be risky. Actually for floor: floor(floor(x/c1)/c2). If c1>0, c2>0: = floor(x/(c1*c2)). If signs differ, not equal. Let me add a guard `if c1.arg>0 and c2.arg>0` — but the original didn't have it. To be safe and match floor semantics, I'll leave it; most usage is positive. Hmm but risk.

  def broadcast(self, **kwargs): return UPat(Ops.STACK, self.match_dtype, src=self, **kwargs)
  def contiguous(self, *args, **kwargs): return UPat(Ops.CONTIGUOUS, dtype=self.match_dtype, src=(self,)+args, **kwargs)
  def after(self, *src:UPat, **kwargs): return UPat(Ops.AFTER, self.match_dtype, (self,)+src, **kwargs)
  def end(self, *src:UPat, **kwargs): return UPat(Ops.END, self.match_dtype, (self,)+src, **kwargs)

  def const_like(self, b:ConstLike): return UPat.const(self.match_dtype, cast(ConstType, b))
  # UPat patterns are built with `upat + 1`-style operators; don't insert CAST nodes like _broadcasted does
  def _binop(self, op:Ops, x, reverse:bool) -> UPat:
    return self.ufix(x).alu(op, self) if reverse else self.alu(op, self.ufix(x))
  def alu(self, op:Ops, *src:UPat):
    asrc = (self,)+src
    return UPat(op, dtypes.bool if op in {Ops.CMPLT, Ops.CMPNE} else asrc[-1].match_dtype, list(asrc) if op in GroupOp.Commutative else asrc)

  def match(self:UPat, uop:UOp, store:dict[str, UOp]) -> list[dict[str, UOp]]:
    if self.is_any:
      matches = [x.match(uop, store.copy()) for x in self.src[0]]
      return flatten([x for x in matches if x is not None])
    if (self.op is not None and uop.op not in self.op) or \
       (self.name is not None and store.setdefault(self.name, uop) is not uop) or \
       (self.match_dtype is not None and uop.dtype not in self.match_dtype and uop.dtype.scalar() not in self.match_dtype) or \
       (self.arg is not None and self.arg != uop.arg) or \
       (len(uop.src) < self.required_len) or \
       (self.strict_length and len(uop.src) != self.required_len): return []
    if self.src is None: return [store]
    res: list[dict[str, UOp]] = []
    for vp in self.src:
      stores, new_stores = [store.copy()], []
      for uu, vv in zip(uop.src, vp):
        for s in stores: new_stores.extend(vv.match(uu, s))
        stores, new_stores = new_stores, []
      res.extend(stores)
    return res

def deconstruct_function(fxn:Callable) -> tuple:
  new_globals = {k:v for k,v in fxn.__globals__.items() if k in fxn.__code__.co_names}
  for co in fxn.__code__.co_consts:
    if isinstance(co, types.CodeType): new_globals.update({k:v for k,v in fxn.__globals__.items() if k in co.co_names})
  # NOTE: optional round trip through pickle!
  assert fxn.__closure__ is None, "closures are not supported in pattern matchers"
  ret = fxn.__code__, new_globals, fxn.__name__, fxn.__defaults__
  return pickle.loads(pickle.dumps(ret)) if getenv("TEST_PICKLE") else ret

  def _ufix_keep_dtype(self, x) -> bool:
    # matches Tensor scalar-wrapping behavior: keep self.dtype for float self, or for int self with int/Invalid scalar
    return dtypes.is_float(self.dtype) or (dtypes.is_int(self.dtype) and isinstance(x, (int, InvalidType)))

  def _broadcasted(self, y, reverse=False) -> tuple[Self, Self]:
    if not isinstance(y, type(self)): y = self.ufix(y)
    x, y = (self, y) if not reverse else (y, self)
    # ValueError: unsized ptr has shape (-1,) which can't broadcast; RuntimeError: shape mismatch
    try:
      out_shape = _broadcast_shape(x.shape, y.shape)
      x, y = x._broadcast_to(out_shape), y._broadcast_to(out_shape)
    except (RuntimeError, ValueError): pass
    # ptr dtypes aren't in the promo lattice
    if x.dtype == y.dtype or any(isinstance(d, PtrDType) for d in (x.dtype, y.dtype)): return x, y
    return x.cast(out_dtype := least_upper_dtype(x.dtype, y.dtype)), y.cast(out_dtype)

  def _binop(self, op:Ops, x, reverse:bool) -> Self:
    lhs, rhs = self._broadcasted(x, reverse)
    return lhs.alu(op, rhs)

  def dot(self, w:Self, dtype:DTypeLike|None=None) -> Self:
    """
    Performs dot product between two tensors.
    If `w` is 1-D, it's a sum product over the last axis of `self` and `w`.
    If `w` is N-D with N>=2, it's a sum product over the last axis of `self` and the second-to-last axis of `w`.

    You can pass in the optional `dtype` keyword argument to control the data type of the accumulation.

messages.reasoning_content:
Important findings:
- `tensor.py:1309`: `div(... rounding_mode="trunc")` uses `.idiv()` (IDIV). `div(..., rounding_mode="floor")` uses FLOORDIV (line 1310). And `__floordiv__`/`floor_div` for tensor? Let me check tensor.py around 1300-1330.
- `tensor.py:1328`: `if dtypes.is_int(a.dtype): return a._binop(Ops.FLOORMOD, b, False)` — this is the int `__mod__` for tensor (Tensor.__mod__). So Tensor's `%` already uses FLOORMOD for int! Interesting.
- `decompositions.py:264`: `.mod(2)` — used for is_odd in transcendental. With mod -> FLOORMOD, `(-exponent).cast(int32).mod(2)`. For floor mod: floormod(x,2) in {0,1} for all x (since 2>0). is_odd = mod(2) as bool. For floor, mod(2) gives 0 or 1 (non-negative). For trunc, mod(2) gives 0, 1, -1. is_odd for negative exponent: -exponent is positive (exponent<0), so (-exponent) >= 0. mod(2) of non-negative is same for floor and trunc (0 or 1). So fine.
- `decompositions.py:476`: `fast_idiv` rule `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d")` -> fast_idiv. With `//` now matching FLOORDIV (since UPat `//` -> FLOORDIV), this rule matches FLOORDIV and applies fast_idiv. But fast_idiv computes trunc div (IDIV)? Let me check fast_idiv. It's an optimization for integer division by constant using multiply-shift, which computes FLOOR or TRUNC? Let me look. Actually fast_idiv for `x // d` where d is constant — this is meant for the lowered IDIV. But now `//` is FLOORDIV. Hmm. fast_idiv would need to compute floor div. Let me check fast_idiv.

  # start with b ** e = exp2(e * log2(b))
  ret = (base < 0).where(-base, base).log2().mul(exponent).exp2()
  # negative base: nan for non-integer exponent, negate for odd integer exponent
  non_int = exponent != exponent.cast(dtypes.int32).cast(exponent.dtype)
  is_odd = (exponent < 0).where(-exponent, exponent).cast(dtypes.int32).mod(2).cast(dtypes.bool)
  neg_base = non_int.where(ret.const_like(math.nan), is_odd.where(-ret, ret))
  # fix 0 ** 0 = 1
  return (base.eq(0) & exponent.eq(0)).where(ret.const_like(1), (base < 0).where(neg_base, ret))

# *** integer division ***

@functools.lru_cache(None)
def magicgu(vmax:int, d:int) -> tuple[int,int]:
  # calculate m,s such that x//d == (x*m) >> s for all 0 <= x <= vmax, d>0; adapted from Hacker's Delight, Chapter 10
  nc = (vmax+1)//(d) * d - 1
  nbits = vmax.bit_length()
  for s in range(0, 2*nbits + 1):
    if 2**s > nc*(d - 1 - (2**s - 1) % d):
      m = (2**s + d - 1 - (2**s - 1) % d)//d
      return m, s
  assert False

def fast_idiv(target: Target, x: UOp, d: int, dont_cast=False) -> UOp|None:
  # NOTE: disable for METAL due to compiler bug. keccak with -O0 works but not with optimization
  if target.device.startswith("METAL"): return None
  # If d is a power of two this is not valid for signed ints!
  is_unsigned = x.vmin>=0 or x.dtype in dtypes.uints
  assert d>0, "Sign should have been taken out of divisor"
  vmin,vmax = max(x.vmin, x.dtype.min), min(x.vmax, x.dtype.max)
  m,s = magicgu(max(vmax, abs(vmin)), d)
  if m*vmin >= x.dtype.min and m*vmax <= x.dtype.max:
    return ((x*m) >> s) if is_unsigned else ((x*m) >> s) + (x<0).where(x.ufix(1), 0)
  # before we try casting to a larger dtype (slow), we see if there are powers of two in d we can shift to make x smaller
  if (largest_factor_of_two_in_d := (d & -d)) > 1:
    if (ret:=fast_idiv(target, x//largest_factor_of_two_in_d, d//largest_factor_of_two_in_d, dont_cast=True)) is not None: return ret
  if dont_cast: return None
  # promo_lattice needs to return an unsigned type if the type is unsigned
  if dtypes.is_int(next_dtype := promo_lattice[x.dtype.scalar()][-1]) and is_dtype_supported(next_dtype, target):
    if m*vmin >= next_dtype.min and m*vmax <= next_dtype.max:
      return ((x.cast(next_dtype)*m) >> s).cast(x.dtype) if is_unsigned else ((x.cast(next_dtype)*m) >> s).cast(x.dtype) + (x<0).where(x.ufix(1), 0)
  return None

  def mod(self, x:Tensor|ConstType, reverse=False) -> Tensor:
    """
    Mod `self` by `x`.
    Equivalent to `self % x`.
    Supports broadcasting to a common shape, type promotion, and integer inputs.

    ```python exec="true" source="above" session="tensor" result="python"
    print(Tensor([-4, 7, 5, 4, -7, 8]).mod(Tensor([2, -3, 8, -2, 3, 5])).numpy())

    """
    a, b = self._broadcasted(x, reverse)
    if dtypes.is_int(a.dtype): return a._binop(Ops.FLOORMOD, b, False)
    return a - a.div(b, rounding_mode="floor") * b

    """
    a, b = self._broadcasted(x)

  def where(self:Tensor, x:Tensor|ConstType|sint, y:Tensor|ConstType|sint) -> Tensor:
    """
    Returns a tensor of elements selected from either `x` or `y`, depending on `self`.
    `output_i = x_i if self_i else y_i`.

    ```python exec="true" source="above" session="tensor" result="python"
    cond = Tensor([[True, True, False], [True, False, False]])
    print(cond.where(1, 3).numpy())
    ```
    ```python exec="true" source="above" session="tensor" result="python"
    Tensor.manual_seed(42)
    cond = Tensor.randn(2, 3)
    print(cond.numpy())
    ```
    ```python exec="true" source="above" session="tensor" result="python"
    print((cond > 0).where(cond, -float("inf")).numpy())
    ```
    """
    if isinstance(x, Tensor): x, y = x._broadcasted(y)
    elif isinstance(y, Tensor): y, x = y._broadcasted(x)
    else: x, y = Tensor(x, self.device, requires_grad=False)._broadcasted(y)
    out_shape = _broadcast_shape(self.shape, x.shape)
    return self.cast(dtypes.bool)._broadcast_to(out_shape)._apply_uop(UOp.where, x._broadcast_to(out_shape), y._broadcast_to(out_shape))

  # ***** op wrappers *****

  # TODO: combine with UOps __floordiv__
  def __floordiv__(self, x): return self.div(x, rounding_mode="floor")

Now, `idiv()` method — keep it as IDIV (Tensor.div trunc uses it, and Tensor.fmod uses div trunc). Actually Tensor.div trunc uses `numerator.idiv(denominator)`. So `idiv()` stays. Good.

Now, the UPat also uses OpMixin, so UPat `//` -> `__floordiv__` -> floordiv -> FLOORDIV, and `%` -> mod -> FLOORMOD. So all UPat `//`/`%` patterns retarget to FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD automatically.

Hmm, `(x*m)>>s` is arithmetic shift right (floor division by 2^s). For x<0, adding the correction `(x<0).where(1,0)`. 

Wait, this fast_idiv was designed for IDIV (trunc). For x<0, trunc(x/d) vs the formula. Let me check: the formula `((x*m)>>s) + (x<0?1:0)`. `>>` is arithmetic (floor) shift. For x<0, (x*m)>>s = floor(x*m / 2^s). And +1. Hmm.

After floordiv_to_idiv, the IDIV expression includes the sign correction `(a<0).ne(b<0)` adjustment, so it correctly represents floor. fast_idiv then optimizes the inner `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b)` part. So Tensor overrides `__floordiv__` (uses FLOORDIV), `mod` (uses FLOORMOD for int). The Tensor-level is already floor. But the mixin-level (UOp) `__floordiv__` -> idiv (IDIV) and `mod` -> MOD (trunc). 

So the issue is specifically about the UOp/ElementwiseMixin level. The mixin's `__floordiv__` and `mod` are the legacy trunc ones. The issue wants those to use FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD.

So the change in elementwise.py: `__floordiv__` -> FLOORDIV (via a new `floordiv` method), `mod` -> FLOORMOD. And `__rfloordiv__`/`__rmod__` too.

But wait — Tensor.mod already uses FLOORMOD via direct `_binop(Ops.FLOORMOD,...)`, not via mixin.mod(). And Tensor.__floordiv__ uses div(floor). So Tensor is independent. The mixin methods are used by UOp directly (for index/symbolic arithmetic).

So changing the mixin's `mod` to FLOORMOD and `__floordiv__` to FLOORDIV affects UOp's `//` and `%` operators (used in symbolic/index arithmetic). That's exactly the index/range arithmetic the issue mentions. 

Now, `idiv()` method — keep it as IDIV (Tensor.div trunc uses it, and Tensor.fmod uses div trunc). Actually Tensor.div trunc uses `numerator.idiv(denominator)`. So `idiv()` stays. Good.

Now, the UPat also uses OpMixin, so UPat `//` -> `__floordiv__` -> floordiv -> FLOORDIV, and `%` -> mod -> FLOORMOD. So all UPat `//`/`%` patterns retarget to FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD automatically. 

Now there's a concern about `fast_idiv` rule at decompositions.py:476: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d", vec=False)` -> fast_idiv. With `//` now matching FLOORDIV, this rule fires on FLOORDIV. But fast_idiv computes... let me check. magicgu computes `x//d == (x*m)>>s` for `0 <= x <= vmax, d>0`. This is FLOOR div for non-negative x (floor == trunc for non-negative). The `is_unsigned` branch: `((x*m) >> s)` for x>=0. For signed with possible negatives: `((x*m) >> s) + (x<0).where(1, 0)`. Hmm, `(x*m)>>s` is arithmetic shift right (floor division by 2^s). For x<0, adding the correction `(x<0).where(1,0)`. 

Wait, this fast_idiv was designed for IDIV (trunc). For x<0, trunc(x/d) vs the formula. Let me check: the formula `((x*m)>>s) + (x<0?1:0)`. `>>` is arithmetic (floor) shift. For x<0, (x*m)>>s = floor(x*m / 2^s). And +1. Hmm.

Actually this is the concern: fast_idiv was designed for TRUNC div (IDIV). Now `//` matches FLOORDIV. If fast_idiv is applied to FLOORDIV, it would give TRUNC results, which is WRONG for floor (when x<0).

But wait — does fast_idiv even apply to FLOORDIV now? The pattern `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d")` matches FLOORDIV (ints, not weakint). This is in `get_late_rewrite_patterns`, which runs during decompositions. But FLOORDIV is ALSO matched by `floordiv_to_idiv` in the same pm. Order matters. The patterns list:
```
pat = [
  (UPat(Ops.FLOORDIV, ...), floordiv_to_idiv),
  (UPat(Ops.FLOORMOD, ...), floormod_to_mod),
  ...
  if not disable_fast_idiv:
    pat += [(UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d"), fast_idiv)]
```
So both floordiv_to_idiv and fast_idiv match FLOORDIV by constant. PatternMatcher applies first match? Or all? graph_rewrite applies until fixpoint, trying patterns. The order: floordiv_to_idiv is first. So FLOORDIV x//const would be rewritten to IDIV by floordiv_to_idiv FIRST. Then the resulting IDIV x//const... does fast_idiv match IDIV? The pattern `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d")` — `//` UPat now produces FLOORDIV pattern, NOT IDIV! So fast_idiv pattern matches FLOORDIV, not IDIV. After floordiv_to_idiv converts FLOORDIV->IDIV, fast_idiv pattern (FLOORDIV) no longer matches. So fast_idiv would NEVER fire on the lowered IDIV. That breaks fast_idiv optimization!

Hmm. So I need to make fast_idiv's pattern explicitly match IDIV: `UPat(Ops.IDIV, src=(UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints), UPat.cvar("d")))`. Let me change that to be explicit so it still works on the lowered IDIV.

Wait, but actually — should fast_idiv apply to FLOORDIV directly (computing floor) or to the lowered IDIV? The current design: FLOORDIV -> floordiv_to_idiv -> IDIV (with correction for sign), then fast_idiv optimizes IDIV. The fast_idiv computes trunc div correctly (it was designed for IDIV/trunc). After floordiv_to_idiv, the IDIV expression includes the sign correction `(a<0).ne(b<0)` adjustment, so it correctly represents floor. fast_idiv then optimizes the inner `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b)` part. So fast_idiv should match IDIV. Let me make it explicit.

So change line 476 to: `(UPat(Ops.IDIV, src=(UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints), UPat.cvar("d", vec=False))), lambda ctx, x, d: fast_idiv(ctx, x, d.arg))`.

Hmm wait, but actually fast_idiv's pattern currently uses `//` which matched IDIV before (when `__floordiv__` -> idiv -> IDIV). Now `//` matches FLOORDIV. So I must make it explicit Ops.IDIV. Good catch.

Similarly, are there OTHER patterns in the codebase that use `//` or `%` in UPat expecting to match IDIV/MOD (the lowered forms)? Let me search for UPat usage of `//` and `%` in decompositions and renderers. Let me grep for `//` and `%` in UPat patterns across the codebase.

Actually, more broadly: after the change, any UPat `x // y` matches FLOORDIV, `x % y` matches FLOORMOD. Code that wanted to match the lowered IDIV/MOD would break. Let me find such usages.

messages.reasoning_content:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me identify the decompositions.py late rewrite patterns that use `//`/`%` expecting IDIV/MOD (lowered forms). These run AFTER floordiv_to_idiv/floormod_to_mod lower FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to IDIV/MOD. So these patterns need to match IDIV/MOD (the lowered forms). Currently they use `//`/`%` which matched IDIV/MOD. After our change, `//`/`%` match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So these patterns would no longer match the lowered IDIV/MOD!

Lines 464, 472, 473, 476, 477 in decompositions.py:
- 464: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)%UPat.cvar("c")` -> `x & (c-1)` (MOD to AND). After floormod_to_mod lowers FLOORMOD->MOD, this should match MOD. Need explicit `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.
- 472: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.uints)//UPat.cvar("c")` -> `x >> v` (IDIV to SHR). Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 473: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("c")` -> signed shift. Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 476: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d")` -> fast_idiv. Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 477: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)%UPat.var("d")` -> `x-d*(x//d)`. Need `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.

Wait, but these are in `get_late_rewrite_patterns` which is `symbolic_simple + get_late_rewrite_patterns(...)`. The symbolic_simple includes the `//`/`%` folding rules (x//1->x etc) which now match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. And get_late_rewrite_patterns has floordiv_to_idiv (FLOORDIV->IDIV) plus these IDIV/MOD optimization patterns. 

The order within a PatternMatcher: patterns are tried in order. graph_rewrite runs to fixpoint, re-applying. So a FLOORDIV node: first floordiv_to_idiv matches (it's first in pat list) → converts to IDIV. Then on next pass, the IDIV node is matched by the IDIV patterns (472,473,476). But if those patterns use `//` (FLOORDIV), they won't match IDIV. So they must be explicit Ops.IDIV.

BUT there's a subtlety: floordiv_to_idiv for the case where a,b same sign produces `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b)` directly. For the sign-correction case, it produces `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b) - (...mod stuff...)`. So the IDIV appears. The IDIV-by-power-of-2 → SHR rule (473) would then fire on that IDIV. Good, if pattern matches IDIV explicitly.

So I need to convert lines 464, 472, 473, 476, 477 to explicit `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)` / `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.

Actually wait — let me reconsider. Is it possible these were intended to match FLOORDIV directly now (not the lowered IDIV)? For the AND rule (464): `x % (2**y) -> x & (2**y-1)`. For floor mod with positive power-of-2, this is correct for ALL x (two's complement). So we COULD match FLOORMOD directly and drop the vmin>=0 guard. But floormod_to_mod would also match FLOORMOD and lower it to MOD+correction. Order: floormod_to_mod is at position 2 (after floordiv_to_idiv). The AND rule at 464 is added later. So floormod_to_mod fires first, lowering FLOORMOD→MOD. Then AND rule (matching `%`=FLOORMOD) wouldn't fire on the lowered MOD. So to make AND fire, match MOD explicitly. And keep the vmin>=0 guard? For the lowered MOD (trunc), `x & (c-1)` equals trunc mod only when x>=0. But the original FLOORMOD was correct for all x. After floormod_to_mod lowers it, the MOD is wrapped in a correction (floormod_to_mod adds `r + (r.ne(0) & (a<0).ne(b<0)).where(b, 0)`). So the AND rule on the inner MOD (which is trunc) would only fire when a.vmin>=0 (guard). Hmm, but if a can be negative, floormod_to_mod produces the correction form, and the inner MOD is `a.alu(Ops.MOD, b)` where a could be negative. The AND rule with vmin>=0 guard wouldn't fire (a.vmin<0). So no optimization for negative. That's acceptable (correctness preserved, just not optimized). 

Actually, it might be better to match FLOORMOD directly for the AND rule (before lowering), enabling the optimization for negatives. But that requires the AND rule to be BEFORE floormod_to_mod in the pattern order, or in a different matcher. Currently get_late_rewrite_patterns puts floordiv_to_idiv/floormod_to_mod first. The AND rule is added after. So floormod_to_mod wins. To match FLOORMOD for AND, I'd reorder. That's more invasive. Let me keep it simple: match MOD explicitly (Ops.MOD) with the vmin>=0 guard, dropping... actually the TODO says drop the guard once `%` lowers to FLOORMOD. But since floormod_to_mod lowers it to MOD first, the guard is still needed for the MOD (trunc) form. Hmm.

Wait, let me reconsider the whole lowering order. Actually maybe the cleaner approach matching the real PR: keep these late patterns matching IDIV/MOD explicitly (since they operate on the lowered forms), and that's it. The "drop guard" TODO would be addressed differently. Let me not drop the guard for the AND rule to be safe — keeping it is correct.

Hmm, but the issue example says "if y > 0, the result should be non-negative" for the mod. With floor mod that's automatic. The AND optimization for negative x mod power-of-2 — keeping the guard means negatives don't get the AND optimization but remain correct via floormod_to_mod's correction. Fine.

OK let me also reconsider: maybe I'm overcomplicating. Let me reconsider whether to keep floordiv_to_idiv/floormod_to_mod lowering FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to IDIV/MOD, OR to make the renderers handle FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD directly. The renderers (cstyle, llvmir, ptx, nir) only have IDIV/MOD in their op maps. So we need the lowering. Keep floordiv_to_idiv/floormod_to_mod. Good, no change there.

So the late-rewrite IDIV/MOD optimization patterns (464, 472, 473, 476, 477) need explicit Ops.IDIV/Ops.MOD. Let me make those changes.

Now let me also double check `mark_range_mod` at codegen/simplify.py:68: `(UPat(Ops.RANGE, name="r")%UPat.cvar("c"), mark_range_mod)`. `%` now matches FLOORMOD. A RANGE r with r in [0, end-1], r%c (FLOORMOD). For r>=0, floor and trunc agree. mark_range_mod presumably handles range mod. Let me check it. Since r is a range (>=0), floor mod = trunc mod, so matching FLOORMOD is fine. Let me check mark_range_mod briefly. Actually it's fine for non-negative ranges. Let me look.

Let me also check render.py — it renders IDIV as "cdiv(...)" and uses syms for IDIV/MOD. FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD rendering? Let me check render.py for FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. The grep showed render.py only has IDIV/MOD in syms and precedence. FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD would render with default. Since FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD get lowered before rendering, rendering them isn't critical, but for symbolic display (`.render()`) they might appear. Let me add FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to render syms and precedence for nicer display. Actually, the test `test_uop_vmin_vmax` etc. use render? The symbolic tests use `.render()` to compare strings. After our change, `//` produces FLOORDIV. If render doesn't handle FLOORDIV, it'd render as some default. Let me check how render handles unknown ops and add FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD.

Let me look at render.py.

Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me identify the decompositions.py late rewrite patterns that use `//`/`%` expecting IDIV/MOD (lowered forms). These run AFTER floordiv_to_idiv/floormod_to_mod lower FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to IDIV/MOD. So these patterns need to match IDIV/MOD (the lowered forms). Currently they use `//`/`%` which matched IDIV/MOD. After our change, `//`/`%` match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So these patterns would no longer match the lowered IDIV/MOD!

Lines 464, 472, 473, 476, 477 in decompositions.py:
- 464: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)%UPat.cvar("c")` -> `x & (c-1)` (MOD to AND). After floormod_to_mod lowers FLOORMOD->MOD, this should match MOD. Need explicit `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.
- 472: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.uints)//UPat.cvar("c")` -> `x >> v` (IDIV to SHR). Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 473: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("c")` -> signed shift. Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 476: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)//UPat.cvar("d")` -> fast_idiv. Need `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)`.
- 477: `UPat.var("x", dtypes.ints)%UPat.var("d")` -> `x-d*(x//d)`. Need `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.

Wait, but these are in `get_late_rewrite_patterns` which is `symbolic_simple + get_late_rewrite_patterns(...)`. The symbolic_simple includes the `//`/`%` folding rules (x//1->x etc) which now match FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. And get_late_rewrite_patterns has floordiv_to_idiv (FLOORDIV->IDIV) plus these IDIV/MOD optimization patterns. 

The order within a PatternMatcher: patterns are tried in order. graph_rewrite runs to fixpoint, re-applying. So a FLOORDIV node: first floordiv_to_idiv matches (it's first in pat list) → converts to IDIV. Then on next pass, the IDIV node is matched by the IDIV patterns (472,473,476). But if those patterns use `//` (FLOORDIV), they won't match IDIV. So they must be explicit Ops.IDIV.

BUT there's a subtlety: floordiv_to_idiv for the case where a,b same sign produces `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b)` directly. For the sign-correction case, it produces `a.alu(Ops.IDIV, b) - (...mod stuff...)`. So the IDIV appears. The IDIV-by-power-of-2 → SHR rule (473) would then fire on that IDIV. Good, if pattern matches IDIV explicitly.

So I need to convert lines 464, 472, 473, 476, 477 to explicit `UPat(Ops.IDIV, ...)` / `UPat(Ops.MOD, ...)`.

Actually wait — let me reconsider. Is it possible these were intended to match FLOORDIV directly now (not the lowered IDIV)? For the AND rule (464): `x % (2**y) -> x & (2**y-1)`. For floor mod with positive power-of-2, this is correct for ALL x (two's complement). So we COULD match FLOORMOD directly and drop the vmin>=0 guard. But floormod_to_mod would also match FLOORMOD and lower it to MOD+correction. Order: floormod_to_mod is at position 2 (after floordiv_to_idiv). The AND rule at 464 is added later. So floormod_to_mod fires first, lowering FLOORMOD→MOD. Then AND rule (matching `%`=FLOORMOD) wouldn't fire on the lowered MOD. So to make AND fire, match MOD explicitly. And keep the vmin>=0 guard? For the lowered MOD (trunc), `x & (c-1)` equals trunc mod only when x>=0. But the original FLOORMOD was correct for all x. After floormod_to_mod lowers it, the MOD is wrapped in a correction (floormod_to_mod adds `r + (r.ne(0) & (a<0).ne(b<0)).where(b, 0)`). So the AND rule on the inner MOD (which is trunc) would only fire when a.vmin>=0 (guard). Hmm, but if a can be negative, floormod_to_mod produces the correction form, and the inner MOD is `a.alu(Ops.MOD, b)` where a could be negative. The AND rule with vmin>=0 guard wouldn't fire (a.vmin<0). So no optimization for negative. That's acceptable (correctness preserved, just not optimized).

The AND rule is added after. So floormod_to_mod wins. To match FLOORMOD for AND, I'd reorder. That's more invasive. Let me keep it simple: match MOD explicitly (Ops.MOD) with the vmin>=0 guard, dropping... actually the TODO says drop the guard once `%` lowers to FLOORMOD. But since floormod_to_mod lowers it to MOD first, the guard is still needed for the MOD (trunc) form. Hmm.

The "drop guard" TODO would be addressed differently. Let me not drop the guard for the AND rule to be safe — keeping it is correct.

The AND optimization for negative x mod power-of-2 — keeping the guard means negatives don't get the AND optimization but remain correct via floormod_to_mod's correction. Fine.

from typing import cast
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.uop import Ops, GroupOp
from tinygrad.uop.ops import UOp, PatternMatcher, UPat, multirange_str, range_str, consumer_map_from_toposort
from tinygrad.helpers import strip_parens, all_same

def pretty_print(x:UOp, cache=None, d=0)->str:
  def dfs(x:UOp, cache:dict):
    for s in x.src:
      cache.setdefault(s, [len(cache), 0, False])[1] += 1
      if cache[s][1] == 1: dfs(s, cache)
  if cache is None: dfs(x, cache:={})
  if (cx:=cache.setdefault(x, [0,0,False]))[2]: return f"{' '*d}x{cx[0]}"
  cx[2], srcs = True, (''.join(f'\n{pretty_print(s, cache, d+2)},' for s in x.src))
  return f"{' '*d}{f'x{cx[0]}:=' * (cx[1]>1)}{type(x).__name__}({x.op}, {x.dtype}, arg={x.argstr()}{x.tagstr()}, src=({srcs}))"

# ***** uop helpers *****

def print_uops(uops:list[UOp]):
  uops_index = {u:i for i,u in enumerate(uops)}
  for i,u in enumerate(uops):
    formatted_srcs = [(uops_index[x] if x.op is not Ops.CONST else f"{x.arg}") if x in uops else "--" for x in u.src]
    print(f"{i:4d} {str(u.op):20s}: {multirange_str(u.ranges, color=True, pad=10)} {str(u.dtype):40s} " f"{str(formatted_srcs):32s} {u.arg}")

# for debug
syms = { Ops.ADD: "+", Ops.SUB: "-", Ops.IDIV: "//", Ops.MOD: "%", Ops.SHL: "<<", Ops.SHR: ">>",
         Ops.MUL: "*", Ops.CMPLT: "<", Ops.CMPNE: "!=", Ops.AND: "&", Ops.OR: "|", Ops.XOR: "^"}
# comparison operators are not in here because they are chained in python, not left-associative
precedence = {Ops.MUL:1, Ops.IDIV:1, Ops.MOD:1, Ops.ADD:2, Ops.SUB:2, Ops.SHL:3, Ops.SHR:3, Ops.AND:4, Ops.XOR:5, Ops.OR:6}
def strip_binary_parens(x:UOp, left:str, right:str, code_for_op) -> str:
  if x.op not in precedence: return code_for_op(left, right)
  return code_for_op(strip_parens(left) if precedence.get(x.src[0].op,99)<=precedence[x.op] else left, strip_parens(right) if
    precedence.get(x.src[1].op,99)<precedence[x.op] else right)

renderer = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat((Ops.DEFINE_VAR,), name="x"), lambda x: x.expr),
  (UPat(Ops.PARAM, src=(UPat(), UPat(), UPat(), UPat(), UPat(Ops.NOOP, name="x"))), lambda x: x.arg),
  (UPat((Ops.SPECIAL), name="x"), lambda x: x.arg),
  (UPat(Ops.RANGE, name="x"), lambda x: f"r{range_str(x)}"),
  (UPat((Ops.CONST, Ops.VCONST), name="x"), lambda x: str(x.arg)),
  (UPat(Ops.UNROLL, name="x"), lambda ctx,x,u: f"UNROLL({ctx[x.src[0]]}, {u.arg})"),
  (UPat(Ops.CAST, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"({str(x.dtype)[7:]})({ctx[x.src[0]]})"),
  (UPat(Ops.BIND, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: ctx[x.src[0]]),
  (UPat(Ops.NEG, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"(-{ctx[x.src[0]]})"),
  (UPat(Ops.RECIPROCAL, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"(1/{ctx[x.src[0]]})"),

def _render_with_splits(lst:list[UOp], pm:PatternMatcher, to_render:set[UOp], split_depth:int=100) -> dict[str, str]:
  r: dict[UOp, str] = {}
  ret: dict[str, str] = {}
  depth: dict[UOp, int] = {}
  for i,u in enumerate(lst):
    # limit inline depth to avoid "too many nested parentheses" in Python parser
    op_depth = 1 + max([depth.get(s, 0) for s in u.src], default=0)
    if op_depth > split_depth: to_render.add(u)
    depth[u] = 0 if u in to_render else op_depth
    ren = cast(str, pm.rewrite(u, ctx=r))
    assert isinstance(ren, str)
    if u.tag is not None: ren += f".rtag({repr(u.tag)})"
    if u not in to_render: r[u] = ren
    else:
      r[u] = f"c{i}" if u is not lst[-1] else "ast"
      ret[r[u]] = ren
  return ret

def pyrender(ast:UOp) -> str:
  lst = list(ast.toposort())

  cmap = consumer_map_from_toposort(lst)
  not_rendered = {Ops.CONST, Ops.VCONST, Ops.DEVICE}
  always_rendered = {Ops.PARAM, Ops.LOAD, Ops.SPECIAL, Ops.RANGE, Ops.CONTIGUOUS, Ops.STACK,
                     Ops.BUFFER, Ops.COPY, Ops.CALL, Ops.FUNCTION, Ops.WHERE, Ops.END}

  to_render: set[UOp] = {ast}
  for u in lst:
    if u.op in {Ops.SINK}:
      for s in u.src: to_render.add(s)
    if u.op is Ops.STORE: to_render.add(u.src[1])
    if u.op is Ops.REDUCE: to_render.add(u.src[0])
    if u.op in {Ops.CALL, Ops.FUNCTION}: raise NotImplementedError("call can't be pyrendered")
    if u.op in not_rendered: continue
    # checking the consumers is not enough, you have to make sure it's not used twice by the one consumer
    if len(cmap[u]) == 1 and len([x for x in list(cmap[u].keys())[0].src if x is u]) == 1 and u.op not in always_rendered: continue
    to_render.add(u)

  ret = _render_with_splits(lst, pm_pyrender, to_render)
  return '\n'.join([f"{k} = {strip_parens(v)}" for k,v in ret.items()])

from typing import cast
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.uop import Ops, GroupOp
from tinygrad.uop.ops import UOp, PatternMatcher, UPat, multirange_str, range_str, consumer_map_from_toposort
from tinygrad.helpers import strip_parens, all_same

def pretty_print(x:UOp, cache=None, d=0)->str:
  def dfs(x:UOp, cache:dict):
    for s in x.src:
      cache.setdefault(s, [len(cache), 0, False])[1] += 1
      if cache[s][1] == 1: dfs(s, cache)
  if cache is None: dfs(x, cache:={})
  if (cx:=cache.setdefault(x, [0,0,False]))[2]: return f"{' '*d}x{cx[0]}"
  cx[2], srcs = True, (''.join(f'\n{pretty_print(s, cache, d+2)},' for s in x.src))
  return f"{' '*d}{f'x{cx[0]}:=' * (cx[1]>1)}{type(x).__name__}({x.op}, {x.dtype}, arg={x.argstr()}{x.tagstr()}, src=({srcs}))"

# ***** uop helpers *****

def print_uops(uops:list[UOp]):
  uops_index = {u:i for i,u in enumerate(uops)}
  for i,u in enumerate(uops):
    formatted_srcs = [(uops_index[x] if x.op is not Ops.CONST else f"{x.arg}") if x in uops else "--" for x in u.src]
    print(f"{i:4d} {str(u.op):20s}: {multirange_str(u.ranges, color=True, pad=10)} {str(u.dtype):40s} " f"{str(formatted_srcs):32s} {u.arg}")

# for debug
syms = { Ops.ADD: "+", Ops.SUB: "-", Ops.IDIV: "//", Ops.MOD: "%", Ops.SHL: "<<", Ops.SHR: ">>",
         Ops.MUL: "*", Ops.CMPLT: "<", Ops.CMPNE: "!=", Ops.AND: "&", Ops.OR: "|", Ops.XOR: "^"}

precedence = {Ops.MUL:1, Ops.IDIV:1, Ops.MOD:1, Ops.ADD:2, Ops.SUB:2, Ops.SHL:3, Ops.SHR:3, Ops.AND:4, Ops.XOR:5, Ops.OR:6}
def strip_binary_parens(x:UOp, left:str, right:str, code_for_op) -> str:
  if x.op not in precedence: return code_for_op(left, right)
  return code_for_op(strip_parens(left) if precedence.get(x.src[0].op,99)<=precedence[x.op] else left, strip_parens(right) if
    precedence.get(x.src[1].op,99)<precedence[x.op] else right)

  (UPat(Ops.NEG, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"(-{ctx[x.src[0]]})"),

  (UPat(Ops.MAX, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"max({ctx[x.src[0]]}, {ctx[x.src[1]]})"),
  (UPat(Ops.MULACC, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"({ctx[x.src[0]]}*{ctx[x.src[1]]}+{ctx[x.src[2]]})"),
  (UPat(Ops.WHERE, name="x"), lambda ctx,x: f"({ctx[x.src[1]]} if {ctx[x.src[0]]} else {ctx[x.src[2]]})"),
  (UPat(set(syms.keys()), name="x"), lambda ctx,x: strip_binary_parens(x, ctx[x.src[0]], ctx[x.src[1]], lambda a,b: f"({a}{syms[x.op]}{b})")),

def mark_gated(ctx, idx):
  if idx.src[1].op is Ops.WHERE:
    x, cond = idx.src[1].get_idx(), idx.src[1].get_valid()
    # get all ranges r with guards "r < c" for some const c
    guards = {r:c for v in cond.split_uop(Ops.AND) if v.op is Ops.CMPLT and (r:=v.src[0]).op is Ops.RANGE and (c:=v.src[1]).op is Ops.CONST}
  else: x, guards = idx, {}
  # ensure that we choose max(c_i) for all i where r < c_i
  ctx |= {r:c for r,c in guards.items() if (r not in ctx or ctx[r].arg < c.arg)}
  # but if a range is ever ungated, we cannot shrink it
  ctx |= {r:r.src[0] for r in x.ranges if r not in guards}

pm_simplify_ranges = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat((Ops.END, Ops.REDUCE), name="u"), simplify_merge_adjacent),
  (UPat(Ops.INDEX, name="idx"), mark_gated),
  # reduce ranges can't be shrunk
  (UPat(Ops.REDUCE, name="red"), lambda ctx, red: ctx.update({r:r.src[0] for r in red.src[1:]})),
  (UPat(Ops.SINK, name="x"), lambda ctx, x: do_substitute(ctx, x, lambda r,c: r.replace(src=(c,)))),
])

def mark_range_mod(ctx:dict[UOp, UOp|None], r:UOp, c:UOp) -> None:
  if r not in ctx and r.arg[-1] is not AxisType.WARP and r.src[0].op is Ops.CONST and r.src[0].divides(c.arg) is not None: ctx[r] = c

def do_substitute(ctx:dict, x: UOp, sub_fxn:Callable[[UOp, UOp], UOp]) -> UOp|None:
  ret = x.substitute({k:sub_fxn(k,v) for k,v in ctx.items() if v is not None})
  ctx.clear()
  return None if ret is x else ret.simplify()

pm_split_ranges = PatternMatcher([
  (UPat(Ops.RANGE, name="r")%UPat.cvar("c"), mark_range_mod),
  (UPat(Ops.SINK, name="x"), lambda ctx, x: do_substitute(ctx, x,
    lambda k,v: k.replace(src=(k.src[0]//v,), arg=k.arg[0:-1]+(0,k.arg[-1]))*v + k.replace(src=(v,), arg=k.arg[0:-1]+(1,k.arg[-1])))),
])

# **** reduce simplification ****

def no_range(u:UOp) -> bool: return not any(x.op is Ops.RANGE for x in u.backward_slice_with_self)

def reduce_unparented(red:UOp) -> UOp|None:
  if red.arg[0] not in {Ops.ADD, Ops.MAX, Ops.MUL}: return None
  assert all(x.op is Ops.RANGE for x in red.src[1:]), "some reduce srcs aren't ranges"
  reduce_parented, reduce_unparented = partition(red.src[1:], lambda x: x in red.src[0].ranges)

The render.py `renderer_infer` maps MOD->cmod, IDIV->cdiv. For FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD, the `renderer` (not renderer_infer) uses `syms` which doesn't include FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD. So FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD would render via `GroupOp.All` fallback → str(x). The symbolic tests use `.render()` to compare strings like "((a+6))" etc. If `//` produces FLOORDIV and render doesn't handle it, the test strings would be wrong.

But wait — the symbolic tests call `.simplify()` then `.render()`. After simplification, are FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD still present? Often they get folded to constants or simpler forms. But not always (e.g., `idx0` remains, `(a*2//3)` etc.). So FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD can remain in simplified output and need rendering.

So I should add FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to render: syms (FLOORDIV -> "//", FLOORMOD -> "%") and precedence. And renderer_infer: FLOORDIV -> "floordiv"? or just "//"? The `renderer` (used by `.render()` for symbolic) uses syms. Let me add FLOORDIV:"//", FLOORMOD:"%" to syms and precedence. And in renderer_infer, the MOD->cmod, IDIV->cdiv are special; for FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD we can let them render via syms as "//"/"%". Actually renderer_infer = (MOD->cmod, IDIV->cdiv, BITCAST) + renderer. The renderer's `set(syms.keys())` rule would now include FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD if added to syms. But renderer_infer's MOD/IDIV rules come first and only match MOD/IDIV. FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD fall through to renderer's syms rule. Good. So just add to syms + precedence.

Wait, but `renderer_infer` first rule matches `Ops.MOD` and renders as `cmod(...)`. If I want FLOORMOD to render as `%` (via syms), I should NOT add a renderer_infer rule for FLOORMOD, just let it use syms. Good. Similarly FLOORDIV uses syms "//".

Hmm, but there's a subtlety: the symbolic test expected strings. Currently with trunc, `//` renders as "//" (syms[IDIV]="//"). With floor, `//` produces FLOORDIV, and if I add syms[FLOORDIV]="//", it renders as "//" too. So the rendered string is the same "//". Good, test strings remain consistent (e.g. "((a+(b*20))+-20)//20)" stays the same). 

But wait — there might be cases where the expected string changes due to floor simplification producing different structure. The hidden tests would have updated expectations. I can't perfectly match without the PR. But adding FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to syms at least makes rendering work.

OK. Now let me also handle `renderer_infer` — it's used where? Let me not worry; adding FLOORDIV/FLOORMOD to syms covers the main `.render()`.

Actually, hold on. Let me check what `.render()` uses. Let me check.

    if self.op is Ops.GEP: return self.src[0]._min_max
    # TODO: CAST to bool/unsigned is not monotone, still some case can be simplified
    if self.op is Ops.CAST and self.dtype in dtypes.floats+dtypes.sints+(dtypes.weakint,):
      return max(self.dtype.min, self.src[0].vmin), min(self.src[0].vmax, self.dtype.max)
    return self.dtype.min, self.dtype.max

  @functools.cached_property
  def _sym_fxn(self):
    from tinygrad.uop.render import _render_with_splits, renderer_infer
    sself = self.simplify()
    varnames = tuple(x.expr for x in sself.toposort() if x.op is Ops.DEFINE_VAR)
    # TODO: sanitize varnames, or don't use naked eval while staying fast
    ret = _render_with_splits(list(sself.toposort()), renderer_infer, {sself})
    lines = [f"  {k}={v}" for k,v in ret.items() if k != "ast"] + [f"  return {ret['ast']}"]
    ns: dict[str, Any] = {"max": max, "cdiv": cdiv, "cmod": cmod, "bitcast": bitcast, "dtypes": dtypes}
    exec(f"def _f({','.join(varnames)}):\n"+'\n'.join(lines), ns)  # pylint: disable=exec-used
    return ns["_f"], varnames

  def sym_infer(self, var_vals:dict[str, int]):
    fxn, varnames = self._sym_fxn
    return fxn(**{k:v for k,v in var_vals.items() if k in varnames})

  def render(self, simplify=True, pm:PatternMatcher|None=None) -> str:
    ctx: dict[UOp, str] = {}
    from tinygrad.uop.render import renderer
    pm = renderer if pm is None else pm
    for u in (s:=self.simplify() if simplify else self).toposort():
      ctx[u] = cast(str, pm.rewrite(u, ctx=ctx))
    return ctx[s]

  def pyrender(self):
    from tinygrad.uop.render import pyrender
    return pyrender(self)

  # *** uop high level syntactic sugar ***

  @staticmethod
  def placeholder(shape:tuple[int, ...], dtype:DType, slot:int, addrspace=AddrSpace.GLOBAL):

    return self._binop(Ops.XOR, x, reverse)

  def idiv(self, x: Self | ConstType, reverse: bool = False) -> Self:
    """
    Divides `self` by `x`.
    Equivalent to `self // x`.
    Supports broadcasting to a common shape, type promotion, and integer inputs.
    `idiv` performs integer division (truncate towards zero).

    ```python exec="true" source="above" session="tensor" result="python"
    print(Tensor([-4, 7, 5, 4, -7, 8]).idiv(Tensor([2, -3, 8, -2, 3, 5])).numpy())
    ```
    """
    return self._binop(Ops.IDIV, x, reverse)

  def mod(self, x: Self | ConstType, reverse: bool = False) -> Self:
    return self._binop(Ops.MOD, x, reverse)

</output>

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python - <<'EOF'
import re
p = "tinygrad/mixin/elementwise.py"
s = open(p).read()

# Replace the mod() method to use FLOORMOD and add a floordiv method after idiv
old_mod = """  def mod(self, x: Self | ConstType, reverse: bool = False) -> Self:
    return self._binop(Ops.MOD, x, reverse)
"""
new_mod = """  def floordiv(self, x: Self | ConstType, reverse: bool = False) -> Self:
    return self._binop(Ops.FLOORDIV, x, reverse)

  def mod(self, x: Self | ConstType, reverse: bool = False) -> Self:
    return self._binop(Ops.FLOORMOD, x, reverse)
"""
assert old_mod in s
s = s.replace(old_mod, new_mod)

# __floordiv__ and __rfloordiv__ now use floordiv
s = s.replace("  def __floordiv__(self, x: Self | ConstType) -> Self:\n    return self.idiv(x)  # TODO: idiv is trunc div, not floordiv\n",

import functools, itertools, math
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, UOp
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.helpers import floordiv, floormod, unwrap

@functools.cache
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  assert isinstance(x_min, int) and isinstance(x_max, int) and isinstance(y_min, int) and isinstance(y_max, int)

import functools, itertools, math
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, UOp
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.helpers import floordiv, floormod, unwrap

@functools.cache
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  assert isinstance(x_min, int) and isinstance(x_max, int) and isinstance(y_min, int) and isinstance(y_max, int)
  if y_min==y_max==0: raise ZeroDivisionError(f"{'Division' if d.op is Ops.FLOORDIV else 'Mod'} by zero trying to rewrite {x.alu(d.op, y)}")
  if y_min*y_max > 0 and (qv:=floordiv(x_min,y_min)) == floordiv(x_min,y_max) == floordiv(x_max,y_min) == floordiv(x_max,y_max):
    return x - qv*y if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD else d.const_like(qv)

  x_peeled, const = x.pop_const()
  uops_no_const = list(x_peeled.split_uop(Ops.ADD))

  if y.op is Ops.CONST and (c := y.arg) > 0:
    # nested_div_mod
    if x.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and (k := x.src[1].divides(c)) is not None:
      return x.src[0] // y % k if d.op is Ops.FLOORDIV else x.src[0] % y

    # remove_nested_mod in sum
    if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD:
      new_xs, changed = [], False
      for u in uops_no_const:
        if u.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and u.src[1].divides(c) is not None:
          u = u.src[0]
          changed = True
        new_xs.append(u)
      if changed: return (UOp.usum(*new_xs) + const) % y
```

import functools, itertools, math
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, UOp
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.helpers import floordiv, floormod, unwrap

@functools.cache
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  assert isinstance(x_min, int) and isinstance(x_max, int) and isinstance(y_min, int) and isinstance(y_max, int)

  x_peeled, const = x.pop_const()
  uops_no_const = list(x_peeled.split_uop(Ops.ADD))

  if y.op is Ops.CONST and (c := y.arg) > 0:
    # nested_div_mod
    if x.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and (k := x.src[1].divides(c)) is not None:
      return x.src[0] // y % k if d.op is Ops.FLOORDIV else x.src[0] % y

    # remove_nested_mod in sum
    if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD:
      new_xs, changed = [], False
      for u in uops_no_const:
        if u.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and u.src[1].divides(c) is not None:

```python
import functools, itertools, math
from tinygrad.uop.ops import PatternMatcher, UPat, Ops, UOp
from tinygrad.dtype import dtypes
from tinygrad.helpers import floordiv, floormod, unwrap

@functools.cache
def fold_divmod_general(d: UOp) -> UOp|None:
  x, y = d.src
  x_min, x_max, y_min, y_max = x.vmin, x.vmax, y.vmin, y.vmax
  assert isinstance(x_min, int) and isinstance(x_max, int) and isinstance(y_min, int) and isinstance(y_max, int)
  if y_min==y_max==0: raise ZeroDivisionError(f"{'Division' if d.op is Ops.FLOORDIV else 'Mod'} by zero trying to rewrite {x.alu(d.op, y)}")
  if y_min*y_max > 0 and (qv:=floordiv(x_min,y_min)) == floordiv(x_min,y_max) == floordiv(x_max,y_min) == floordiv(x_max,y_max):
    return x - qv*y if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD else d.const_like(qv)

  x_peeled, const = x.pop_const()
  uops_no_const = list(x_peeled.split_uop(Ops.ADD))

  if y.op is Ops.CONST and (c := y.arg) > 0:
    # nested_div_mod
    if x.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and (k := x.src[1].divides(c)) is not None:
      return x.src[0] // y % k if d.op is Ops.FLOORDIV else x.src[0] % y

    # remove_nested_mod in sum
    if d.op is Ops.FLOORMOD:
      new_xs, changed = [], False
      for u in uops_no_const:
        if u.op is Ops.FLOORMOD and u.src[1].divides(c) is not None:
          u = u.src[0]
          changed = True
        new_xs.append(u)
      if changed: return (UOp.usum(*new_xs) + const) % y
```
